pr.title: Fix SetScaleHack by making HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty a macro.
pr.author: RevoSucks

summary: Replaced the inline HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty helper in jobj.h with a preprocessor macro, allowing fighter.c to remove a local HSD_JObjSetScale_Hack workaround and call the normal HSD_JObjSetScale path. The PR notes this affected at least two functions and also HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll because previous matching required manual inlining. The important decomp insight was that HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty appears to behave like a macro in the original build rather than a normal inline function, even though that is unusual relative to neighboring HSD_JObj helpers.

key_files.role: Changed HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty from an inline function under always_inline pragmas into a macro and removed the surrounding pragma push/pop.
Removed the local HSD_JObjSetScale_Hack workaround and restored Fighter_UpdateModelScale to call HSD_JObjSetScale.

what_changed: Removed `#pragma push`, `#pragma always_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` around the inline HSD_JObj helper region in `include/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h`.
Converted `inline void HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty(HSD_JObj* jobj)` into `#define HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty(jobj) { ... }` with the same null-check, dirty-check, and `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub(jobj)` call body.
Deleted the `HSD_JObjSetScale_Hack` inline workaround from `src/melee/ft/fighter.c`, including its manually inlined dirty-matrix logic.
Kept an explanatory uncertainty comment in the header: `Why does this seem to be a define while the others are inline functions?`
smart_moves: Fixed the matching problem at the shared header abstraction instead of preserving a fighter-specific hack.
Used a macro to reproduce the apparent call-site expansion behavior needed by multiple functions, rather than forcing one manually inlined copy in `fighter.c`.
Removed duplicated hand-inlined matrix-dirty logic once the underlying helper expanded correctly.
Recognized that the local `HSD_JObjSetScale_Hack` was compensating for incorrect inline depth and that the better fix was to model `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty` more accurately.
decomp_lessons: If several functions only match when a helper is manually inlined, the original SDK/header construct may have been a macro rather than an inline function.
Do not solve shared inline-depth problems with per-file hacks if a header-level macro or inline declaration better explains multiple call sites.
Adjacent helpers in a header do not necessarily share the same implementation form; one JObj helper may be a macro while surrounding helpers remain inline functions.
Removing artificial `always_inline` pressure can be part of matching when the actual source model is selective macro expansion rather than blanket forced inlining.
A workaround comment about compiler inline depth is useful evidence, but once a cleaner header model is found, prefer deleting the workaround to avoid fossilized fake APIs.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty
naming_conventions.lesson: Kept the SDK-style exported helper name but implemented it as a macro, preserving call-site source spelling.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_JObjSetScale_Hack
naming_conventions.lesson: Temporary decomp workaround names with `_Hack` should be removed when the real header behavior is understood.

naming_conventions.name: getHSDJObj
naming_conventions.lesson: Unaffected local helper in `fighter.c`; no naming feedback was provided for it in this PR.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Modeled `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty` as a macro to force expansion at the call site without relying on compiler inline heuristics.
Removed manual expansion from `HSD_JObjSetScale_Hack` after the macro made the normal `HSD_JObjSetScale` call path produce the desired code.
Addressed a known inline-depth issue where automatic inlining preferred a depth that did not match the target assembly.
The PR body states the change also affects `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll`, implying the macro fixed more than the visible `fighter.c` workaround.

review_feedback.feedback: A reviewer asked whether the change allowed the workaround at `include/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h#L147` to be removed.
review_feedback.outcome: The author replied `Yes.` and the diff removed the pragma-based workaround region.

follow_up_queries: Find other JObj helper functions that only match when manually inlined and check whether they should be macros.
Search for remaining `_Hack` helpers that duplicate SDK header logic and may be removable via better macro or inline modeling.
Inspect `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll` matching history to confirm exactly how the `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty` macro affected it.
Audit uses of `#pragma always_inline on` in headers to see whether they are masking more precise original macro behavior.
